# README Inspiration Patterns

These notes distill the style and structure signals from the referenced example READMEs:

- Azure-Samples/serverless-chat-langchainjs
- Azure-Samples/serverless-recipes-javascript
- sinedied/run-on-output
- sinedied/smoke

Use these as direction, not as a literal template.

## Repeating Patterns Worth Reusing

- A clean header with project name and often a small logo or icon.
- A short positioning sentence immediately under the header.
- Optional badges, but kept relevant to runtime, build status, package version, or docs.
- A compact navigation line or quick links near the top when the README is long enough to justify it.
- A strong Overview or Features section early.
- Clear Getting Started and Quickstart steps with runnable commands.
- Links to deeper docs instead of stuffing advanced detail into the README.
- Screenshots or diagrams only when they help orientation.

## Tone Guidance

- Professional and friendly, not inflated.
- Direct and explanatory.
- Optimized for someone landing on the GitHub page for the first time.
- Confident without overselling.

## Content Guidance

- Lead with what the project is and why it matters.
- Explain the major building blocks only once.
- Prefer short feature bullets over huge tables.
- Show one or two practical commands early.
- Keep troubleshooting or support links short and actionable.

## What to Avoid

- Huge badge walls that dominate the page.
- Long legal, contribution, or changelog sections when those already live in separate files.
- Repeating the same selling points in multiple sections.
- Dumping the entire docs tree into the README.
- Decorative emoji overload.

## Header Guidance

- If the project has a logo, use it near the title.
- Prefer one hero image or one logo, not several competing visuals.
- For technical OSS repos, simple often reads better than elaborate.

## Section Heuristics

Use a subset of these based on the repository:

- Overview
- Features
- Getting Started
- Installation
- Quickstart
- Usage
- Project Structure or Architecture
- Documentation
- Troubleshooting

Skip sections that would just repeat dedicated standalone documents.
